title: DoorDash says will begin offering hourly pay to its drivers
text: DoorDash Inc (NYSE:DASH) has said it will begin offering hourly pay to its delivery drivers in an effort to address concerns about unpredictable earnings. Under the new payment model, known as Dashers, drivers will have the option to earn a prorated hourly minimum rate in addition to tips.

title: DoorDash offers delivery workers hourly rate, but there's a catch
text: DoorDash will give delivery workers the option to be paid a guaranteed hourly minimum rate instead of being paid per delivery, the company said Wednesday.

title: DoorDash Adds Hourly Pay Option for Gig Economy Drivers
text: DoorDash will now let its gig workers choose to be hourly workers. The aggregator will let drivers choose to earn a minimum hourly rate, to use the existing method in which they are paid per offer, and to switch from one to the other, DoorDash said in a Wednesday (June 28) press release.

title: 10 Years and 5 Billion Orders Later, DoorDash Expands to More On-Demand Categories
text: In DoorDash's decade of operation, the company has taken the restaurant aggregator business model and expanded it to so much more, shifting its goals from food delivery to providing everything on demand.

title: DoorDash is offering hourly pay to fix a problem with food orders
text: Some DoorDash drivers are fed up with the unpredictable delivery business: Some orders can take longer than expected due to circumstances outside of workers' control. The company said it has a solution.

title: DoorDash doubles down on ‘earn by time,' which gives delivery workers option of guaranteed minimum pay
text: DoorDash is going all in on a big change for its delivery workers: the ability to choose whether they want to keep earning the old way — per offer — or by time, with minimum guaranteed hourly wages.

title: Your DoorDash driver? He's the company's co-founder
text: On a recent afternoon in San Francisco, a DoorDash driver was circling the neighborhood—first in his car, then on foot—trying to find the restaurant where he needed to pick up two orders. Finally, he Googled the location and realized DoorDash's app sent him to the wrong address.

title: DoorDash Adds SNAP EBT Acceptance to Win Low-Income Shoppers' Grocery Loyalty
text: In an effort to gain share of consumers' grocery spending both from traditional brick-and-mortar grocers and from aggregator rivals such as Instacart, DoorDash is expanding its audience by adding the ability to pay via Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT).

title: McDonald's: A Strategic Awakening for Long-Term Growth
text: Embarking on a transformative maneuver, McDonald's Corp. ( MCD , Financial) is looking to reignite its growth by enhancing the customer experience.

title: 3 Stocks Down 54% to 88% I'm Not Taking Into the Next Bull Market
text: Wall Street professionals are currently debating whether stocks have entered a new bull market. Regardless, investors should always focus on buying quality companies with strong prospects at an attractive price.

title: DOUBLE DEAL-IVERY ALERT: WENDY'S AND DOORDASH DOUBLE DOWN ON DELIVERY DEALS
3 Stable Stocks to Own Now
text: Successful investing is more than just looking for the next hot stock. These three stocks offer strong balance sheetsm growth, and steadiness.

title: DoorDash: Valuation Seems Unjustified
text: DoorDash stock has risen over 20% since late April, with the company reporting better-than-expected Q1 results and raising guidance. Despite concerns about consumer spending, food delivery orders continue to surge, with DASH holding the #1 market share in the US restaurant delivery business. However, the company's high valuation, extensive use of stock-based compensation, and potential vulnerability to consumer spending cutbacks raise concerns about its future growth.

title: How opera workers' ruling could help Uber, Lyft and DoorDash workers unionize
text: A federal labor board ruling Tuesday on worker classification could make it easier for app-based gig workers to unionize.

title: Uber and DoorDash warn of huge changes when New York City adopts its new $18 minimum wage for delivery workers
text: New York City announced a new law making $18 the minimum wage for delivery workers. DoorDash and Uber worry the law will hamper flexibility and reduce the delivery workforce.

title: Harnessing the Power of Long-Term Technical Analysis
text: Bear markets are brutal for investors. However, savvy investors can turn crisis into opportunity using this long-term signal to gain an edge.

title: New York City announces minimum wage for app food delivery workers
text: New York City on Sunday announced a new minimum pay-rate for app food delivery workers amid a rise in use of services like UberEats and DoorDash since the pandemic.

title: DoorDash and Edible Team to Capture Consumers' Gift Spending
text: DoorDash, in its ongoing effort to find and create new occasions for on-demand delivery, is increasing its gifting options. The aggregator announced Thursday (June 8) a partnership with Edible, the company known for its giftable arrangements of fruits and sweets, to offer on-demand delivery from nearly 1,000 of the latter's locations.

title: DoorDash, Inc. (DASH) Recently Broke Out Above the 20-Day Moving Average
text: DoorDash, Inc. (DASH) is looking like an interesting pick from a technical perspective, as the company reached a key level of support. Recently, DASH crossed above the 20-day moving average, suggesting a short-term bullish trend.
